title: Event Listeners 👂
description: Wait for button clicks and trigger JavaScript functions instantly.

slides.title: Websites Are Interactive 🖱️
slides.content:
# Waiting for the User

Modifying the DOM is cool, but having it happen instantly when the page loads is boring.

We want our JavaScript to only activate *when the user does something*, like clicking a "Buy Now" button or typing in a password field.

We do this by attaching an **Event Listener** to an HTML element.

slides.title: addEventListener() 👂
Putting It Together 🧩
slides.content: We can combine Selectors, Event Listeners, and Modifiers to build a full Interactive App!

quizData.question: What is the very first argument provided to `.addEventListener(?, ?)`?

options.text: A string labelling the specific Event (like 'click' or 'scroll')

quizData.explanation: You must first tell the listening device exactly what it is trying to hear. ('click', 'mouseenter', 'keydown'). The second argument is the function that executes when the sound is heard.

slides.title: The Event Object 🎁

quizData.question: If you want to track a user typing their password into an `<input>` field, what Event Object would you need to inspect to see what letters they pressed?

options.text: The parameter (usually named 'e' or 'event') passed secretly into the callback function

quizData.explanation: The 'e' (Event) object acts like a flight data recorder, containing every single piece of data about that exact millisecond interaction in time.
